top_signals.title: China to Spend $295B on Nationwide AI Buildout | The China Show 6/10/2026
top_signals.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man, David Ingles and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
top_signals.summary: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Self-censorship, insecurity, financial squeeze: a press under pressure in Ivory Coast
top_signals.summary: Press freedom is more established in Ivory Coast than in other west African countries but remains precarious in a region hit by conflict and under political and economic pressure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Apollo's Kleinman Says PE Needs to Start Capitulating on Valuations
top_signals.summary: Apollo Global Management Co-President Scott Kleinman discusses the outlook for the private equity industry. Speaking to Bloomberg's Dani Burger on the sidelines of the SuperReturn conference in Berlin, Kleinman says longer hold periods are hurting internal rates of return (IRRs) and “folks are going to have to start capitulating for sure” on valuations. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Ascot CEO on Royal meet, pooling media rights and the best of Britain
top_signals.summary: The chief executive of Ascot Racecourse has suggested major horse racing meets could pool their media rights to compete with rival sports. The 300-year-old Royal Ascot festival returns to Berkshire next week with record prize money and 300,000 fans expected over five days of racing. top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Fuller’s slams ‘unprecedented government interference’ in pub sector
top_signals.summary: The boss of Fuller’s has slammed Labour for “unprecedented government interference” into the pub sector, which he says is to blame for 5,800 pubs closing in a decade. Simon Emeny, executive chairman of Fuller, Smith and Turner, has launched a broadside against the government’s hikes to business rates, national insurance contributions and alcohol duty.  “When [...]
